subject: If you've been watching the news . . .
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
Friend,

If you've been watching the news, you know September will be a very busy month.

**But if you 're relying on the news to stay up to date, [you probably don't know about the work we've already done to keep our campaign promises.](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MzAzMjM4MDgS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMjg1NTE0MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)**

Did you know**we relieved the regulatory burden on small businesses** by repealing Dodd Frank?

No? Well, did you hear about **our work to rebuild the military?** We increased defense spending and gave our service members their largest pay raise in 8 years.

**How about our work to drain the swamp?** We're cracking down on government agencies like the VA by demanding accountability to better protect our veterans.

Friend, we have a lot of work left to do. But we are moving your agenda forward -- even though the news doesn't always highlight our work.

Conservatives overcame the biased news coverage last November, and our efforts paid off **\-- but we're not done yet. We have an uphill battle ahead of us and we need all of the conservative support we can muster.****

With our August 31st deadline approaching, we need to know you're with us. **** **

**[Chip in $10 >>](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MzAzMjM4MDgS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMjg1NTE0MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Senator Maggie Hassan
subject: Getting dark money out of politics
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello,
Earlier this week, the _Concord Monitor_ published my op-ed on the efforts I am taking, along with many of my senate colleagues, to fix our broken campaign system and get dark money out of politics.
The full piece is below. I know it is long but I hope you will read it and share it so we can build support for these efforts to ensure a transparent and fair campaign finance system, where everyone has a chance to have their voice heard.
_America 's campaign finance system is severely broken, and it is hurting our democracy._
_Every American deserves to know that their voice and their vote is equal to that of their fellow citizens. But today, the political system is captured by corporate special interests that get their way while hard-working families and small businesses pay the price._
_In 2010, the Supreme Court 's misguided _Citizens United _decision reshaped how our political system operates - tipping the scale in favor of powerful corporate interests. That decision helped unleash unlimited dark money in politics, overwhelming the voices of everyday Americans and influencing the policies that are addressed in Washington._
_During my campaign for United States Senate, I pledged to the people of New Hampshire that I would take action to help ensure that money does not control our politics. And that is exactly what I am working to do._
_I recently joined 45 of my colleagues in introducing the DISCLOSE Act of 2017, which would increase transparency in our campaign finance system. This common-sense bill requires any organization that engages in political spending to disclose its major donors -- helping to restore accountability and giving voters a better understanding of who is behind political advertising._
_Another consequence of our broken campaign finance system is that it could actually allow foreign donors, corporations or governments to influence our elections. While current law bans foreign individuals and companies from spending money in U.S. elections, there is a loophole for domestic companies with significant foreign ownership._
_This loophole makes it possible for foreign nationals and foreign companies to use dark money to interfere in our elections. The DISCLOSE Act would work to address this problem by prohibiting domestic corporations under foreign control or ownership from spending money in U.S. elections._
_As we work to pass the DISCLOSE Act, I am also pursuing other avenues to increase transparency in the political system, joining a number of my colleagues in sending a letter to Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Jay Clayton urging the SEC to require public companies to disclose to their shareholders their political spending._
_While transparency is critically important, transparency alone is not enough to fix our broken campaign finance system. That is why I also helped introduce the Fair Elections Now Act, which would reduce the influence of big donors and corporate special interests by creating a voluntary system of public financing for Senate candidates._
_Finally, in order to get at the root of this problem, and ensure that corporate special interests do not control our politics, I helped introduce a constitutional amendment that would reverse the Supreme Court 's decision in _Citizens United.
_Fixing the broken campaign finance system shouldn 't be a partisan issue, and I will work with anyone on either side of the aisle who is serious about standing up to special interests and getting dark money out of politics._
_The future of our democracy depends on it._
Thank you for taking the time to read.
[**If you believe that it 's time to get dark money out of politics, please give $3 or more now to join the fight to end _Citizens United_ and make sure that voters have the power, not corporate special interests.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde37/2a380d31/1f6d1220/fe7f51/2729124633/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDgyNV9FQ1UtT3BFZF9hY3QiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e20170825_ECU-OpEd_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan
[Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde37/2a380d31/1f6d1220/fe7f51/2729124633/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDgyNV9FQ1UtT3BFZF9hY3QiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e20170825_ECU-OpEd_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

name: Tim Kaine
subject: I've been thinking a lot about this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

This week, I've found myself thinking a lot about President Trump's response to the events that took place in Charlottesville. For me, those events were a tragic but powerful reminder that no matter who tries to take us backwards, we must focus on moving ahead -- on continuing Virginia's progress, and on working to ensure that every citizen in our Commonwealth and our country feels safe from violence and hatred. The President's remarks were alarming because he seemed unwilling to call out and genuinely, categorically decry the violent white supremacy that was on gruesome display.

**The fact is, when it comes to racist violence and white supremacy, there are not "very fine people" on "both sides." And while we need to call out violence wherever it exists, to suggest there is some sort of moral equivalence is outrageous. When there is an act of terrorism, when white supremacists commit acts of violence that result in the deaths of innocent Virginians, you have to condemn the intolerable.**

**[Add your name today if you agree that we ALL have a responsibility to condemn the intolerable.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=izyJDEHp2e4ZXsPoYJxbTS6nkXVdP9NK)**

Every one of us must commit to condemn hatred. To call out bigotry and racism, wherever and whenever we see it. And together, we must commit to put in the hard work required to create that more perfect union that we know is possible.

Thank you.

Tim

name: Tom Perez
subject: Derek, this is who Trump has always been:
body:
Step up before our deadline on Thursday.
---
**We need 89 Democrats from Manassas to step up before Thursday if we're going to hit our August fundraising goal. Can you chip in $3 or more today?

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405237eb/4067b0a/3db4debc0/7e6fa65c/1961011373/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/August-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170825_tp_nd&source=em_20170825_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170825_tp_nd")**

Derek, Donald Trump has had a lot to say lately. One day he reads a prepared statement about unifying the country, and the next day he's attacking anyone who disagrees with him.

But his actions say so much more than his words ever could.

He ran for office promising a despicable Muslim ban which we are still fighting. He has terrorized immigrant families with a massive increase in deportations -- even DREAMers with legal status have been detained under his watch. He's directed Mike Pence to lead an investigation into "voter fraud," a nonexistent problem, as a guise to disenfranchise who-knows-how-many of us.

Just a few days ago, he threatened to shut down the government unless our tax dollars are spent to build a wall that most Americans don't want, won't make us safer, and is rooted in prejudice. And he all but promised a pardon to the racist former sheriff Joe Arpaio, who was found guilty of violating a court order when he refused to stop racially profiling Latinos.

Donald Trump has not changed in the last couple weeks. This is who he is, who he has always been.

Until we're able to get Donald Trump out of the White House, we need to elect more Democrats at every level of office who can fight back against him -- and we can start doing that in just a few weeks by electing Democratic governors and legislators in Virginia and New Jersey, and winning back a Democratic majority in Washington's state senate.

Help make sure we can keep organizing and mobilizing to win. Pitch in $3 or more before the deadline on Thursday to help us fight Trump with everything we've got.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405237eb/4067b0a/3db4debc0/7e6fa65d/1961011373/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/august-Deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20170825_tp_nd&source=em_20170825_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170825_tp_nd")

[QUICK DONATE: $25](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405237eb/4067b0a/3db4debc0/7e6fa65b/1961011373/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/august-Deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+QUICK+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20170825_tp_nd&source=em_20170825_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170825_tp_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405237eb/4067b0a/3db4debc0/7e6fa658/1961011373/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/august-Deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20170825_tp_nd&source=em_20170825_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170825_tp_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405237eb/4067b0a/3db4debc0/7e6fa659/1961011373/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/august-Deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20170825_tp_nd&source=em_20170825_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170825_tp_nd")

Tom

Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Andrew Gillum
body:
#
Team --
We're one year and three days away from our first election in this race for Florida Governor. Here's what you need to know about the current state of our campaign:
As of right now, we've received 40 endorsements from leaders like former Obama Administration HUD Secretary Julian Castro, U.S. House Representatives Frederica Wilson and Alcee Hastings, more than three dozen elected officials across Florida, and former FAMU President Frederick S. Humphries. We've also been endorsed by Democracy for America, one of the largest and most effective progressive organizations in the nation.
Thanks to supporters like you, we're leading in the number of contributions to our campaign. So far, we've received more than 9,000 individual contributions to our campaign online at an average contribution of just $51.50. [**If you 'd like to join them in supporting our campaign, you can do so right here, right now >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32b8/9e00690/4cfb4794/2403695d/3548384751/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017?refcode=em20170825-eom")
Still, there are many pundits and politicos who doubt our chances. They don't believe it's possible for us to win this race because I don't have a famous name or the ability to stroke myself a big check. They believe I'm too bold, that I'm too progressive. But I disagree.
Our winning strategy doesn't involve passing the hat around the corrupt special interest groups or compromising our values for campaign checks. Instead, we're focusing on fueling this campaign with people-power and talking about the issues that matter most to Floridians in every corner of the state.
[**Help us make this campaign and this movement possible by chipping in $3.68, or whatever you can afford, before our end of month fundraising deadline >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32b8/9e00690/4cfb4794/2403695d/3548384751/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017?refcode=em20170825-eom")
We're focused on finding solutions to ensure every Floridian has access to quality, affordable health care -- no matter what our President does. We're working with teachers and parents to figure out what our schools and students need to succeed, like more vocational training and less high-stakes testing -- and figuring out how we can give teachers the pay raise they deserve. We're planning ways to create good paying jobs in Florida that will also work to combat the harsh reality of climate change -- jobs in solar power and clean energy infrastructure. We're fighting for equal rights for all, and together, I believe we can move this state forward.
[**But we can 't do it alone. Will you contribute $3.68, or whatever you can afford, to ensure we have the resources to spread our message across the state?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32b8/9e00690/4cfb4794/2403695d/3548384751/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017?refcode=em20170825-eom")
I know we can count on you. Thank you for making this possible.
Bring it home,
Andrew

**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32b8/9e00690/4cfb4794/2403695c/3548384751/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017?refcode=em20170823-famu")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32b8/9e00690/4cfb4794/2403695f/3548384751/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32b8/9e00690/4cfb4794/2403695e/3548384751/VEsA/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32b8/9e00690/4cfb4794/24036951/3548384751/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

name: just-launched@dscc.org
subject: Can these 10 female Democratic Senators count you in? (Say you're IN!)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
We're launching the 2018 Women's Senate Network, featuring house parties across the country. Will you be a part of it?
---
**[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d9f1/2b07114c/1576653/7313fd17/2366948208/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/womens-senate-network-house-party?source=PT-20170825-JustLaunched_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")**
Team, nearly HALF of the women of the Senate are up for re-election next fall.

We need to help all of these Senators launch strong, tough campaigns - and that means starting grassroots fundraising and organizing NOW.

**That 's why we're holding a National Day of Action for the Women's Senate Network on September 23.**

With help from supporters like you, our National Day of Action for the Women's Senate Network will feature house parties across the country full of grassroots Democrats who are determined to keep all ten of the Democratic female Senators in the Senate.

**Will you sign up to host a Women 's Senate Network house party on our National Day of Action?**

[**Yes, I 'm on board to host a house party to help launch a grassroots movement for the 2018 Women's Senate Network on Saturday, September 23.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d9f1/2b07114c/1576653/7313fd17/2366948208/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/womens-senate-network-house-party?source=PT-20170825-JustLaunched_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

[**I can 't host a party, but I'd like to contribute $1 or more to give the Women's Senate Network the resources they need in the fights ahead.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20170825-JustLaunched_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

And don't worry, we'll even send you a host kit to make sure your party is a success!

From health care to Planned Parenthood, our Democratic women Senators have been at the forefront of some of the toughest fights in the Senate -- but their willingness to take on these tough fights has made them top targets in 2018.

That's why organizing to reach your friends and family is so critical right now. We need to ensure that our Democratic women champions have the resources they need to defeat their Republican challengers in 2018.

And the best way to make sure your friends and family know what's at stake? Invite them to be a part of our Day of Action for the Women's Senate Network!

Thanks for being a part of this effort,

Team DSCC

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d9f1/2b07114c/1576653/7313fd11/2366948208/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d9f1/2b07114c/1576653/7313fd10/2366948208/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d9f1/2b07114c/1576653/7313fd13/2366948208/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: What America does Trump believe in?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
I'm working to protect the America you and I see -- not the one he's fighting for.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTE3Nzg2MDMS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTIyMjMxMzA4NAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, Recently, we watched as white nationalists violently made it known that they do not believe in our nation's promise of inclusion, opportunity, and a better future for ourselves and our children. And President Trump did nothing to admonish them. Instead, the White House cheered them on. Even after all that has happened since the start of the Trump presidency, this was truly shocking. President Trump has been working with his Republican friends to repeal DACA and rip away the American dreams of 800,000 young people. And after the tragedy in Charlottesville, President Trump has only solidified that he is on the side of those who want to build monuments to intolerance and division -- not hope and determination. That's why I've written an op-ed for CNN calling on Congress to protect Dreamers and refute the president's nationalistic and fear-mongering rhetoric. And I'm asking you to share it with your friends and family so they can understand just how crucial this issue is. [**Share my op-ed on Facebook and show Trump we won 't let him take away the DACA program! **](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTE3Nzg2MDMS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTIyMjMxMzA4NAS2&mt=1&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTE3Nzg2MDMS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTIyMjMxMzA4NAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Over 800,000 young people have been granted protection from deportation under DACA. 17,000 of them live in Washington state. These Dreamers are students, teachers, doctors, and small business professionals -- living, working, studying, and raising families. They want to be here, and they make America stronger, not weaker, by contributing to the nation. But President Trump has shown he refuses to protect them -- and now, it's up to Congress. [**Will you share this op-ed calling on Congress to stand up for Dreamers?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTE3Nzg2MDMS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTIyMjMxMzA4NAS2&mt=1&rt=0) You and I know we are stronger together. Now, let's show President Trump. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [READ & SHARE »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTE3Nzg2MDMS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTIyMjMxMzA4NAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

**_Democrat Donnelly's Unbelievable Week_**
Democrat Joe Donnelly's re-election campaign launched this week. That wasn't unbelievable. What was unbelievable though was the series of events that exposed exactly who Democrat Joe Donnelly really is for the whole world to see.

First was the ironic timing of a visit by ultra-liberal Bernie Sanders on the same day Democrat Donnelly announced. Now, you might think that Donnelly and Bernie are far apart on the political spectrum if you believe everything Democrat Donnelly says. But the fact is he and Bernie vote together 79 percent of the time. But that's not all.

In what can only be called a brazen act of political desperation, Democrat Donnelly tried to deflect attention from that fact by claiming that "Trump supporters were originally Joe Donnelly supporters and still are." _That_ is unbelievable!

We know Donald Trump voters and they are not Joe Donnelly voters. That's about as far from the truth as Democrats claiming if we liked our doctors we could keep our doctors.

Bottom line: Democrat Donnelly is a lot closer to Bernie Sanders than Donald Trump. And Hoosiers will know that soon, too.
\- Kyle Hupfer

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I encourage you to watch this video of my trip to IN, OH and MI
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
I recently returned to Burlington after great events in Indianapolis and Detroit, cities that voted overwhelmingly for Hillary Clinton, and Portsmouth, Ohio, a community that voted overwhelmingly for Donald Trump.
At each stop, I met people of all backgrounds who had an enormous amount in common. Whether they voted for Clinton, Trump or me they wanted jobs that paid a living wage, health care that was affordable and the ability to send their kids to college no matter what their income was.
And with the recent events in Charlottesville weighing heavily on everyone's minds, they want to live in a country free of racism and bigotry. Unlike the president, they know there are no "nice" Nazis.
I wanted to go to these three very different communities because our mission is to bring people together in every zip code in the country. At a time when Trump and his allies are trying to divide us up by race, religion, sexual orientation or national origin, our job is to bring people together to create an economy and a government that works for everyone, not just the 1 percent.
We recorded a short video of the trip. I hope that you'll watch it and listen to some of the stories from the tour. Then share it with your friends on social media.
[](https://www.facebook.com/berniesanders/videos/1517157398339259/ "https://www.facebook.com/berniesanders/videos/1517157398339259/") [](http://ctt.ec/B1cNE "http://ctt.ec/B1cNE")
[](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/watch-oh-in-mi?source=em170825-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/watch-oh-in-mi?source=em170825-full")
During the presidential campaign, I made it a point to stop at conservative areas like Liberty University and McDowell County, West Virginia. I did that because it is imperative that we go beyond our political comfort zones, start reaching out to people who do not agree with us on every issue and see where, if possible, we can find common ground. And believe me, there is a lot more common ground out there than the media portrays.
I can tell you definitively that people I have met of all backgrounds -- men and women, gay and straight, black, white, Latinx, Asian and Native American -- are sick and tired of a government and an economy that works overtime for the rich and the powerful while ignoring the needs of working people. Very few people that I have talked to, whether they are Democrats, Republicans or Independents, believe it makes any sense at all to give tax breaks to billionaires while throwing 23 million Americans off of health insurance or privatizing Medicare.
Most people that I have talked to, from Maine to California, believe strongly in the concept of American democracy. They disagree with the Supreme Court decision on Citizens United and do not believe that billionaires like the Koch brothers should be able to buy elections or that governors should mount massive campaigns of voter suppression -- efforts that are intentionally denying people of color, low-income people and students the ability to participate in the political process.
And increasingly, people of all economic backgrounds understand that it is a moral abomination that in the richest country in the history of the world we remain the only developed nation on this earth that does not guarantee health care as a right to every man, woman and child.
I encourage you to watch the video from the tour and see the conversations that took place over the past two days. You can do that here:
[**https://go.berniesanders.com/august-tour**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/watch-oh-in-mi?source=em170825-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/watch-oh-in-mi?source=em170825-full")
In Indianapolis, I joined with Chuck Jones, the former head of the Steelworkers, who stood up to Donald Trump when he lied about saving jobs at the Carrier plant in Indiana and continues to stand up for American workers. I heard T.J. Bray, a Carrier worker whose family, including five aunts and uncles, has more than 125 years of service at the company. He watched as Donald Trump promised to save all their jobs at Carrier and he watched as that promise was broken and 550 of his co-workers were laid off.
In Detroit, I joined Representative John Conyers, one of the great progressive leaders in Congress, who has repeatedly introduced legislation that would ensure health care is a right in this country.
And in Portsmouth, Ohio, I met Zach, who told the story of caring for his nearly three year old daughter who was diagnosed with epilepsy and cerebral palsy. Zach makes $9 an hour and he and his wife rely on Medicaid to take care of their daughter.
As you may know, in the next few weeks I'll be introducing my Medicare-for-all bill. The campaign to pass this legislation will not be easy and it will not be quick. We will be taking on the insurance industry, the drug companies, Wall Street and all those who make billions from our dysfunctional health care system. The only way we win that struggle is when millions of Americans at the grass-roots level, stand up, fight back and make it clear that in this country we believe that health care is a right, not a privilege.
The recent events I attended in Indiana, Ohio and Michigan were great. The turnouts were large and the people there were strong and beautiful. Those who came out to our rallies and town meetings know what you know and what I know. And that is that if people of all backgrounds can come together and fight for economic, social, racial and environmental justice there is nothing we cannot accomplish.
And that is our job. Stand together, fight back and create the great nation that we all know we can become.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
* * *
P.S. As you know, I don't go around asking millionaires and billionaires for money. These trips, and our political revolution, is funded by working people of all backgrounds chipping small amounts of money to power our movement forward. If you can afford it -- and only if you can afford it -- add a $3 donation today: [**https://go.berniesanders.com/august-tour**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-august-tour?refcode=em170825-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-august-tour?refcode=em170825-full")

clean_body: Sisters and Brothers - I recently returned to Burlington after great events in Indianapolis and Detroit, cities that voted overwhelmingly for Hillary Clinton, and Portsmouth, Ohio, a community that voted overwhelmingly for Donald Trump. At each stop, I met people of all backgrounds who had an enormous amount in common. Whether they voted for Clinton, Trump or me they wanted jobs that paid a living wage, health care that was affordable and the ability to send their kids to college no matter what their income was. And with the recent events in Charlottesville weighing heavily on everyone's minds, they want to live in a country free of racism and bigotry. Unlike the president, they know there are no "nice" Nazis. I wanted to go to these three very different communities because our mission is to bring people together in every zip code in the country. At a time when Trump and his allies are trying to divide us up by race, religion, sexual orientation or national origin, our job is to bring people together to create an economy and a government that works for everyone, not just the 1 percent. We recorded a short video of the trip. I hope that you'll watch it and listen to some of the stories from the tour. Then share it with your friends on social media. "" "" "" During the presidential campaign, I made it a point to stop at conservative areas like Liberty University and McDowell County, West Virginia. I did that because it is imperative that we go beyond our political comfort zones, start reaching out to people who do not agree with us on every issue and see where, if possible, we can find common ground. And believe me, there is a lot more common ground out there than the media portrays. I can tell you definitively that people I have met of all backgrounds -- men and women, gay and straight, black, white, Latinx, Asian and Native American -- are sick and tired of a government and an economy that works overtime for the rich and the powerful while ignoring the needs of working people. Very few people that I have talked to, whether they are Democrats, Republicans or Independents, believe it makes any sense at all to give tax breaks to billionaires while throwing 23 million Americans off of health insurance or privatizing Medicare. Most people that I have talked to, from Maine to California, believe strongly in the concept of American democracy. They disagree with the Supreme Court decision on Citizens United and do not believe that billionaires like the Koch brothers should be able to buy elections or that governors should mount massive campaigns of voter suppression -- efforts that are intentionally denying people of color, low-income people and students the ability to participate in the political process. And increasingly, people of all economic backgrounds understand that it is a moral abomination that in the richest country in the history of the world we remain the only developed nation on this earth that does not guarantee health care as a right to every man, woman and child. I encourage you to watch the video from the tour and see the conversations that took place over the past two days. You can do that here: "" In Indianapolis, I joined with Chuck Jones, the former head of the Steelworkers, who stood up to Donald Trump when he lied about saving jobs at the Carrier plant in Indiana and continues to stand up for American workers. I heard T.J. Bray, a Carrier worker whose family, including five aunts and uncles, has more than 125 years of service at the company. He watched as Donald Trump promised to save all their jobs at Carrier and he watched as that promise was broken and 550 of his co-workers were laid off. In Detroit, I joined Representative John Conyers, one of the great progressive leaders in Congress, who has repeatedly introduced legislation that would ensure health care is a right in this country. And in Portsmouth, Ohio, I met Zach, who told the story of caring for his nearly three year old daughter who was diagnosed with epilepsy and cerebral palsy. Zach makes $9 an hour and he and his wife rely on Medicaid to take care of their daughter. As you may know, in the next few weeks I'll be introducing my Medicare-for-all bill. The campaign to pass this legislation will not be easy and it will not be quick. We will be taking on the insurance industry, the drug companies, Wall Street and all those who make billions from our dysfunctional health care system. The only way we win that struggle is when millions of Americans at the grass-roots level, stand up, fight back and make it clear that in this country we believe that health care is a right, not a privilege. The recent events I attended in Indiana, Ohio and Michigan were great. The turnouts were large and the people there were strong and beautiful. Those who came out to our rallies and town meetings know what you know and what I know. And that is that if people of all backgrounds can come together and fight for economic, social, racial and environmental justice there is nothing we cannot accomplish. And that is our job. Stand together, fight back and create the great nation that we all know we can become. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders * P.S. As you know, I don't go around asking millionaires and billionaires for money. These trips, and our political revolution, is funded by working people of all backgrounds chipping small amounts of money to power our movement forward. If you can afford it -- and only if you can afford it -- add a $3 donation today: "" Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, August 25th**
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**
**The disappointment continues; our federal delegation is decidedly not representing New Hampshire in Washington. Maggie Hassan hasn't been there even a year and she's become part of the problem, not the solution. Her strategy is to follow the wrong-headed philiosophy of the Democrat party: obstruct and resist. Hardly what voters sent her to Washington to do. Annie Kuster and Carol Shea-Porter have adopted the same strategy.**

**Jeanne Shaheen just recently endorsed Bernie Sanders' single payer heath care plan--a truly socialist takeover of our health care system. Granite Staters can't afford Obamacare and the 44% increases that come along with it and they surely won't be able to afford Sanderscare.**

**It's comforting at least to know that we have a Governor looking out for us. Governor Sununu has had some real successes here in New Hampshire: a fiscally responsible budget with no new taxes; a record investment and strategy in combating the opioid crisis, and working hard to attract new businesses and support existing businesses by cutting regulation and reducing business taxes.**

clean_body: nh.gop NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, August 25th A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, The disappointment continues; our federal delegation is decidedly not representing New Hampshire in Washington. Maggie Hassan hasn't been there even a year and she's become part of the problem, not the solution. Her strategy is to follow the wrong-headed philiosophy of the Democrat party: obstruct and resist. Hardly what voters sent her to Washington to do. Annie Kuster and Carol Shea-Porter have adopted the same strategy. Jeanne Shaheen just recently endorsed Bernie Sanders' single payer heath care plan--a truly socialist takeover of our health care system. Granite Staters can't afford Obamacare and the 44% increases that come along with it and they surely won't be able to afford Sanderscare. It's comforting at least to know that we have a Governor looking out for us. Governor Sununu has had some real successes here in New Hampshire: a fiscally responsible budget with no new taxes; a record investment and strategy in combating the opioid crisis, and working hard to attract new businesses and support existing businesses by cutting regulation and reducing business taxes. ** Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: Join the Ministers March for Justice
body:
The clergy and people of faith have helped move our country forward
#
Friends,
The clergy and people of faith have helped move our country forward in times of strife and peril, and this Monday we honor the 54th anniversary of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.'s "I Have A Dream" speech here in Washington. In the struggle for voting rights, health care, criminal justice reform, and economic justice, belief in a power greater than ourselves has forged bonds of solidarity amongst disparate groups, bringing people of all denominations together for the common purpose of our greater humanity.
[**Join Reverend Al Sharpton and more than one thousand ministers of every denomination this Monday in Washington for the Ministers March for Justice to honor Dr. King's legacy and continue his struggle for justice and equality.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af99/bed43eb/965c02e/75ff9b6c/2655706014/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/1364064256980871/?source=em170825-ourrev")
**[Ministers March for Justice](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af99/bed43eb/965c02e/75ff9b6c/2655706014/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/1364064256980871/?source=em170825-ourrev")**
Martin Luther King, Jr. National Memorial
1964 Independence Ave SW
Washington, DC 20037
Monday, August 28, 2017
10:00 AM--2:00 PM EDT
**[RSVP»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af99/bed43eb/965c02e/75ff9b6c/2655706014/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/1364064256980871/?source=em170825-ourrev")**
Attorney General Jeff Sessions' Department of Justice and the Trump administration have sought to undermine Dr. King's dream. In the wake of the 2013 Supreme Court decision to gut the Voting Rights Act in Shelby County v. Holder and a renewed assault on our freedoms with mass incarceration, hate crimes, and discrimination, it is imperative that we reaffirm our commitment to justice in all of its forms.
Our march will be led by the clergy and people of faith, and we welcome all who share our desire for the realization of Dr. King's dream for true equality and prosperity. No matter our color, creed, or who we love, every single human being deserves to realize the promise of America.
[**March with us this Monday at the Ministers March for Justice in Washington.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af99/bed43eb/965c02e/75ff9b6c/2655706014/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/1364064256980871/?source=em170825-ourrev")
Let us honor Dr. King's legacy and continue his work on the 54th anniversary of the Dream he shared with our nation.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002
